content_markdown:
A student states that the following factors may lead to global warming

I. decreased albedo of the Earth's surface
II. increase in volcanic activity
III. deforestation.

Which of the above statements are correct?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present in the image.

vibe_explanation: Decreased albedo (I) means less radiation is reflected and more is absorbed, warming the planet. Deforestation (III) reduces the removal of $CO_2$ from the atmosphere, enhancing the greenhouse effect. While volcanic activity (II) releases some greenhouse gases, its primary short-term effect is actually global cooling due to the release of aerosols that reflect incoming solar radiation.
